steps.title: Identify Walsh's core finding
steps.content: The passage states that social dimensions made users “feel more mindful of how their listening activities may be perceived, which in turn influenced how they managed those activities.” The decisive element: users become aware of social observation and actively adjust their listening in response.

steps.title: Find the quote showing perception-driven behavior change
steps.content: Choice B shows the speaker consciously selecting songs based on what “friends who are following me might be interested in” rather than their actual preference, demonstrating the behavior shift Walsh documented. Choices A, C, and D discuss passive streaming, algorithmic recommendations, and social connection, but none capture the self-conscious modification of listening driven by awareness of how others perceive it. That matches choice B — B is correct.
